reason: Finding 1 (fabrication) has medium user impact: the SORT_ORDER note is factually accurate based on the data shown in the table, but it is an AI-generated inference not present in the source documentation. An agent relying on this file would present the inference as official Nablarch-documented behavior, potentially misleading developers about SORT_ORDER guarantees in multilingual environments. The note must be removed. Finding 2 is a false positive with no impact.

findings_assessment.justification: The fabricated note ('SORT_ORDERは言語ごとに異なる場合がある…') is factually inferable from the example data (ID=0001/VALUE=01 has SORT_ORDER=1 in ja and 2 in en), which is why the Phase E fix agent retained it — it did not appear to be an error when cross-checked against the data in the same section. However, the source RST contains no such commentary; the observation is an AI inference presented as documented behavior. An agent using this file could cite it as an official Nablarch design guarantee, misleading developers about SORT_ORDER semantics in production multilingual systems. The note must be removed. Persisted through D→E because the Phase E agent validated the note against the co-located table data and found it consistent, treating accuracy as sufficient justification for inclusion.

Confirmed false positive. The automated check miscounted RST horizontal rule dividers (----) as headings. The knowledge file's 4 sections (s1–s4) correctly map to the 4 actual content subsections of the source. No content is missing or misrepresented. No fix needed; the warning reflects a checker bug, not a knowledge file defect.
